message.content:
Investigate the design of CourtOps Tennis at `C:\Users\vkozy\repos\tennis-app`. Five prior audit passes are documented in `docs/design-audit.md` (all 120 items closed). Most recent commit `6a48bde` consolidated import/export.

Read the audit doc first so you don't re-report closed items. Then look fresh at the actual code as it stands now — backend routers + importer + tests + frontend app.js + util.js + shirts.js + index.html + styles.css.

**Show ONLY incomplete items / open issues / unresolved bugs.** If something works correctly, don't mention it. If everything is fine, say so explicitly and stop — don't pad.

Look hard at:
- Anything that was recently consolidated (roster.py rewrite via importer registry; ESM module conversion; models.py façade split; the 9-type importer registry). Subtle regressions from these are the highest-value find.
- The new merge functions: `_merge_distance`, `_merge_pairing`, `_merge_doubles`. Edge cases the tests don't exercise.
- The new `exportCols` plumbing on `makeListGrid` / `wirePlayerList`. Does it actually fire on every site? Is there a list that has `exportCols` but the column keys don't exist on the row data shape?
- Cross-cutting consistency: do all 9 importer types appear in the UI's import type selector? Are templates downloadable for all 9?
- New tests: do they actually exercise what their names imply? Any assertion that's degenerate?
- Anything that's been touched 5+ times and might have ended up in an internally-inconsistent state.

Limit findings to actual problems — bugs, broken UX, missing wiring, contradictions, dead code. Skip style preferences. Under 800 words. If nothing substantive remains, say "no outstanding issues" in a sentence and stop.

1 # CourtOps Tennis — Design Audit (2026-05-26) 2 3 Audit run against the working tree; each finding lists file paths and concrete 4 fix suggestions. Items checked off below have been addressed in code. 5 6 ## Critical 7 8 - [x] **C1. Roster CSV import silently defaults gender='female'** — `backend/app/routers/roster.py:144` defaults every imported player to `female` to dodge the NOT NULL constraint. Corrupts gender-aware division filtering for boys imports. 9 *Fix:* parse a `gender`/`sex` column from the CSV; reject rows without it. 10 - [x] **C2. No login rate-limit / lockout** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:13-32` accepts unlimited POSTs. Add per-IP+username counter with backoff (e.g. 5 fails → 30s sleep). 11 - [x] **C3. Session cookie missing `secure` flag + no token rotation on login** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:31`. Gate `secure=True` by env so localhost still works; regenerate token on each login. 12 - [x] **C4. Room-block PUT trusts body `tournament_id`** — `frontend/app.js:1491,1506` posts `tournament_id = active.id`; backend currently accepts whatever is sent. Force tournament_id from the URL path on PUT. 13 - [x] **C5. `api()` stringifies FastAPI 422 arrays in toasts** — `frontend/app.js:60-63`. Detail arrays produce `[{"loc":[...]}…]` strings in the UI. Humanize: pick `body.detail[0].msg` when detail is a list. 14 - [x] **C6. `rosterMatches` filters by `JSON.stringify(data)`** — `frontend/app.js:1058-1061,2135-2138`. Typing `123` matches internal `player_id:123`. Restrict to named string fields (name, usta, division, status…). 15 - [x] **C7. `/me` mounted outside admin guard** — `backend/app/main.py:46`. Confirm every endpoint in `me.py` has `get_current_user` dep. Better: wrap router with `Depends(get_current_user)` at mount time so it can't be forgotten. 16 - [x] **C8. Global Escape closes detail modal during cell edit** — `frontend/app.js:646`. Escape inside an active Tabulator editor cancels the cell *and* dismisses the surrounding modal, losing context. Guard with `!document.querySelector('.tabulator-editing')`. 17 - [x] **C9. Cookie `samesite=lax` + no CSRF** — CSRF deferred, but tighten to `strict` to reduce cross-origin mutation risk. 18 - [x] **C10. Hotel confidential popup uses `document.write` with template strings** — `frontend/app.js:2019-2074`. `esc()` doesn't escape ``-style breakouts in meta text. Replace with DOM construction or use the strict `&` escape. 19 20 ## Moderate 21 22 - [x] **M11. `wireEntity` doing too much** — `frontend/app.js:648-890`. 240+ lines: grid, CSV, in-grid edit, prev/next nav, modal, filtering, row actions, submit. Extract `_buildGrid`, `_buildModal`, `_wireNav`, `_wireSubmit`. 23 - [x] **M12. Four grid factories duplicate built/pending dance** — `wireEntity`, `makeListGrid`, `makeReadGrid`, `wirePlayerList` reimplement the deferred-setData pattern. Pull into one `buildGrid(mount, options)`. 24 - [x] **M13. Roster grid duplicates wireEntity master-detail** — `frontend/app.js:947-1162` clones the modal/prev-nav/submit because of its dual pick-vs-create flow. 25 - [x] **M14. `updateActiveUI` eagerly loads 14 panels on every tournament change** — `frontend/app.js:625`. Lazy-load on tab activation (loader map at 540-556 already exists). 26 - [x] **M15. `loadAssignments` does 4 sequential awaits** — `frontend/app.js:1281-1303`. Parallelize sites/blocks/list/avail with `Promise.all`. 27 - [x] **M16. `refreshAllSelects` called by every Setup onLoad** — fires 5× on first paint. 28 - [x] **M17. `api()` always sends `Content-Type: application/json`** — even on GET (and forces FormData uploads to bypass `api()`). 29 - [x] **M18. `api()` `await res.json()` throws on HTML 5xx pages** — surface `status + statusText` on parse error. 30 - [x] **M19. No optimistic concurrency** — two tabs editing the same player silently overwrite. 31 - [x] **M20. `markInvalid` regex over humanized error text is brittle** — read FastAPI `err.loc` directly. 32 - [x] **M21. `expandPlayerRef` leaves orphan fields when cache is stale** — `frontend/app.js:475-481`. 33 - [x] **M22. `renderAssignment` mixes string template + DOM append** — convert to template literal + `replaceChildren`. 34 - [x] **M23. `_outOfWindow` is naive string comparison** — works for valid ISO but `2025-3-1` would break ordering. 35 - [x] **M24. Grid `redraw(true)` on tab-show duplicated four places** — central IntersectionObserver. 36 - [x] **M25. `buildImportPage` uses raw `fetch`** — bypasses `_progress` and error normalization. 37 - [x] **M26. `enhanceSelect` MutationObserver fires per option add** — `frontend/app.js:410-411`. Disconnect during batch fills. 38 - [x] **M27. `syncCombos` via `requestAnimationFrame` repeated in 6 places** — wrap in `withCombos(fn)` helper. 39 - [x] **M28. Shirt sizes hardcoded in 4 files** — `index.html:422-428`, `app.js:993`, `app.js:2083`, `roster.py:79-84`. Source from one place. 40 - [x] **M29. Cert types hardcoded in 3 files** — same problem. 41 - [x] **M30. No max validation on `one_way_miles`** — pasting `99999` succeeds. Add server cap (e.g. 1000) and HTML `max`. 42 - [x] **M31. Form fields aren't disabled while submit is in flight** — `frontend/app.js:297-304`. User can edit while saving. 43 - [x] **M32. Filter inputs don't debounce** — fine for 32 players, breaks at scale. 44 - [x] **M33. Two competing "Work on →" affordances on Tournament Setup** — `frontend/app.js:2487-2502`. Pick one. 45 - [x] **M34. `loadAvailability` re-fills the official select and relies on side-effects** — brittle. 46 47 ## Polish 48 49 - [x] **P35. Setup forms ship with `` in HTML** — JS rewrites to "Cancel" only inside wireEntity; other forms still say "New". 50 - [x] **P36. No grid loading state on initial fetch** — empty placeholder ("No tournaments yet…") is misleading until the API resolves. 51 - [x] **P37. `.needs-active-note` hardcodes `#fff8e6`** — not dark-mode aware. 52 - [x] **P38. `fmtDOW` uses local TZ** — late-night users near UTC boundaries see wrong DOW. 53 - [x] **P39. Toast color always white on accent** — not theme-aware. 54 - [x] **P40. Microcopy mismatch on submit buttons** — "Add player" vs "Update player" vs "Save". Pick one verb. 55 - [x] **P41. Confirm dialog OK button retains previous label class** when reused. 56 - [x] **P42. Inconsistent empty states** — Tabulator placeholder vs hand-built `

` (assignments). 57 - [x] **P43. `?` help button is unlabeled visually** — has aria-label but no tooltip / sighted affordance. 58 - [x] **P44. Focus restoration via DOM node fails when row re-renders** — track by row id. 59 - [x] **P45. `#tshirt-order-table` mixes inline `display:none` with CSS class** — pick one. 60 - [x] **P46. Keyboard shortcut `n` triggers New, but tab-switch is mouse-only** — add `1..9` tab order. 61 62 ## Architectural drift 63 64 - [x] **A47. `app.js` ~2958 lines, three SPAs in one file** — split via native ESM modules (`setup.js`, `workspace.js`, `me.js`, `grids.js`). 65 - [x] **A48. `GRIDS` registry + redraw plumbing is a workaround for layout-while-hidden** — `visibility:hidden` panels may obviate. 66 - [x] **A49. `FILE_TARGETS` + `FORM_MODALS` are parallel registries** of the same set of forms. 67 - [x] **A50. `models.py` ~594 lines** — split by domain (`models/tournament.py`, `models/roster.py`, …). 68 - [x] **A51. `roster.py` carries player-upsert + shirt normalization + history JOIN** — extract into shared modules (`playerops.py` already exists). 69 - [x] **A52. `roster.py` has its own one-off CSV/XLSX import alongside `importer.py`** — consolidate. 70 71 --- 72 73 **Working order:** Critical → Moderate → Polish → Architecture. Items not directly improving correctness/security may be deferred if they require disproportionate refactor; those will be marked as such. 74 75 --- 76 77 ## Second-pass findings (2026-05-26) 78 79 ### Critical regressions / new 80 - [x] **N1.** `playerops.upsert_player` still hardcodes `gender='female'` for inbox flows — C1 only patched roster.py. Inbox-filed boys become female records. 81 - [x] **N2.** `seed.py` cleanup deletes legitimate players with NULL birthdate (any roster-inline-create / inbox upsert) and cascades through 8 child tables. 82 - [x] **N3.** `PlayerCreate.birthdate` required blocks editing pre-existing players with NULL DOB. 83 - [x] **N4.** `set_official_account` `ON CONFLICT (username) DO UPDATE` lets an admin steal `admin` (or any other) account. 84 - [x] **N5.** `update_my_profile` SQL drops `lat`/`lng`. 85 - [x] **N6.** Login rate-limit `_attempts` dict grows unbounded. 86 - [x] **N22.** `LoginIn` username/password not length-capped → pbkdf2 DoS. 87 88 ### Moderate 89 - [x] **N8.** `onSubmit` restores stale `disabled` state from before the handler ran. 90 - [x] **N9.** `onSubmit` disables the Cancel button during submit. 91 - [x] **N10.** `expandPlayerRef` throw leaves modal stuck — should refresh players cache. 92 - [x] **N11.** `_tournamentLoaders` lazy init misses the initial-load case. 93 - [x] **N14.** `loadAssignments` `Promise.all` blanks everything on any single failure. 94 - [x] **N20.** `_datesInRange` parses local TZ (DST drift). 95 - [x] **N21.** `cancelTshirtOrder` doesn't reset cached state synchronously. 96 97 ### Polish 98 - [x] **N24.** HTML still has 3 verbs ("Add player"/"Add official"/"Add block"). 99 - [x] **N28.** `aria-pressed` missing on Roster pick/new toggle. 100 - [x] **N30.** `_humanizeDetail` truncates 50 errors to 3 with no "+ N more" hint. 101 - [x] **N31.** Redundant `e.preventDefault()` inside `onSubmit`-wrapped handlers. 102 - [x] **N33.** Date-format helpers duplicated; pull into one helper. 103 104 --- 105 106 **Won't-fix for POC (engineering cost > value for a single-user demo):** 107 - **M11, M12, M13** — `wireEntity`, `makeListGrid`, `makeReadGrid`, `wirePlayerList` could be consolidated, but the four-factory layout is consistent and the consolidation touches every grid path in the app. Revisit when the app grows to multiple devs. 108 - **M19** — optimistic concurrency requires `updated_at` checks across every PUT site + UI conflict-resolution UX. 59	    for c in cols:
60	        m[c.canon] = c.canon
61	        for a in c.aliases:
62	            m[a] = c.canon
63	    return m

66	def parse_file(filename: str, raw: bytes, cols) -> list[dict]:
67	    """Return [{row_num, data:{canon:val}}] for non-empty rows, mapping headers."""
68	    amap = {_norm(k): v for k, v in _alias_map(cols).items()}
69	    name = (filename or "").lower()
70	    if name.endswith((".xlsx", ".xlsm")):
71	        ws = load_workbook(io.BytesIO(raw), data_only=True, read_only=True).active
72	        it = ws.iter_rows(values_only=True)
73	        headers = next(it, []) or []
74	        rows = list(it)
75	    else:
76	        text = raw.decode("utf-8-sig", errors="replace")
77	        reader = csv.reader(io.StringIO(text))
78	        headers = next(reader, [])
79	        rows = list(reader)
80	    cmap = {i: amap[_norm(h)] for i, h in enumerate(headers) if _norm(str(h) if h is not None else "") in amap}
81	    out = []
82	    for n, r in enumerate(rows, start=1):
83	        rec = {canon: _s(r[i]) if i < len(r) else None for i, canon in cmap.items()}
84	        if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
85	            out.append({"row_num": n, "data": rec})
86	    return out

89	def validate(data: dict, cols, cur) -> str | None:
90	    """Audit F18: `cur` is now required so a future caller can't accidentally
91	    skip the staging-time gender check by forgetting to pass it."""
92	    missing = [c.canon for c in cols if c.required and not _s(data.get(c.canon))]
93	    if missing:
94	        return "missing " + ", ".join(missing)
95	    # Audit T2: surface the gender requirement at *staging* time so the TD
96	    # sees "30 invalid (missing gender)" before clicking Merge, not after.
97	    usta = _s(data.get("usta_number"))
98	    if usta:
99	        cur.execute("SELECT 1 FROM player WHERE usta_number = %s", (usta,))
100	        if cur.fetchone() is None and _norm_gender(data.get("gender")) is None:
101	            return f"player {usta} isn't in Setup yet — gender column is required for new players"
102	    return None

211	    per group, with `usta_1`, `usta_2`, `usta_3?`, `usta_4?` cells. At least
212	    two non-blank USTAs are required. Players must already exist in Setup."""
213	    members = []

215	        u = _s(d.get(f"usta_{n}"))

220	    seen: list[int] = []

277	def _merge_distance(cur, tid, d):
278	    """Setup-catalog importer: official↔site distance (audit import/export #7).
279	
280	    `tid` is unused — distances are a Setup catalog, not per-tournament.
281	    Identifies official + site by id (preferred) or by label lookup:
282	      - official: (last_name, first_name)
283	      - site: code, then name
284	    Inserts new rows or updates the existing (official, site) pair.
285	    """
286	    miles = _s(d.get("one_way_miles"))
287	    if miles is None:
288	        raise ValueError("one_way_miles is required")
289	    try:
290	        miles_f = float(miles)
291	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
292	        raise ValueError(f"one_way_miles must be numeric, got {miles!r}")
293	    # Resolve official.
294	    oid = _coerce_int(d.get("official_id"))
295	    if oid is None:
296	        first, last = _s(d.get("first_name")), _s(d.get("last_name"))
297	        if not last:
298	            raise ValueError("official_id or (first_name, last_name) is required")
299	        if first:
300	            cur.execute(
301	                "SELECT id FROM official WHERE lower(last_name) = lower(%s) "
302	                "AND lower(first_name) = lower(%s)",
303	                (last, first),
304	            )
305	        else:
306	            cur.execute(
307	                "SELECT id FROM official WHERE lower(last_name) = lower(%s)",
308	                (last,),
309	            )
310	        row = cur.fetchone()
311	        if row is None:
312	            raise ValueError(f"official {first or ''} {last} not found")
313	        oid = row["id"]
314	    # Resolve site.
315	    sid = _coerce_int(d.get("site_id"))
316	    if sid is None:
317	        code, name = _s(d.get("site_code")), _s(d.get("site_name"))
318	        if code:
319	            cur.execute("SELECT id FROM site WHERE code = %s", (code,))
320	            row = cur.fetchone()
321	        elif name:
322	            cur.execute("SELECT id FROM site WHERE lower(name) = lower(%s)", (name,))
323	            row = cur.fetchone()
324	        else:
325	            raise ValueError("site_id, site_code, or site_name is required")
326	        if row is None:
327	            raise ValueError(f"site {code or name} not found")
328	        sid = row["id"]
329	    source = _s(d.get("source")) or "manual"
330	    if source not in ("manual", "geocoded"):
331	        source = "manual"
332	    cur.execute("SELECT id FROM official_site_distance WHERE official_id = %s AND site_id = %s", (oid, sid))
333	    existing = cur.fetchone()
334	    conflict = None
335	    if existing:
336	        cur.execute(
337	            "UPDATE official_site_distance SET one_way_miles = %s, source = %s WHERE id = %s",
338	            (miles_f, source, existing["id"]),
339	        )
340	        conflict = "distance already on file — overwritten"
341	    else:
342	        cur.execute(
343	            "INSERT INTO official_site_distance (official_id, site_id, one_way_miles, source) "
344	            "VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s)",
345	            (oid, sid, miles_f, source),
346	        )
347	    return conflict

1	"""Tournament <-> Player roster (tournament_entry) + CSV/XLSX direct-merge import.
2	
3	The direct-merge endpoint here and the staged importer in `importer.py` share a
4	single source of truth: header aliases, parse/validate, and the merge function
5	all live in `importer.py`. This file owns the *immediate-write* UX (no staging
6	review) for the TD's official USTA roster upload, plus the regular CRUD."""
7	import psycopg
8	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, File, HTTPException, Response, UploadFile
9	
10	from .. import importer
11	from ..db import db_dep
12	from ..models import RosterEntryCreate, RosterEntryOut
13	from ..playerops import upsert_player
14	from ..shirtops import norm_shirt as _norm_shirt
15	
16	router = APIRouter(tags=["roster"])
17	
18	
19	@router.post("/api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/players/import")
20	async def import_roster(tournament_id: int, file: UploadFile = File(...), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
21	    """Upload a CSV/XLSX roster; upsert players (by USTA #) and their entries.
22	
23	    Audit (import/export #1): consolidated on `importer.parse_file` +
24	    `importer._merge_roster` so this path can't drift from the staged importer
25	    on header aliases, the gender pre-check, or shirt normalization. The
26	    response shape is preserved for the existing frontend.
27	    """
28	    cfg = importer.TYPES["roster"]
29	    records = importer.parse_file(file.filename, await file.read(), cfg["cols"])
30	    created = updated = upserted = 0
31	    errors: list[str] = []
32	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
33	        cur.execute("SELECT id FROM tournament WHERE id = %s", (tournament_id,))
34	        if cur.fetchone() is None:
35	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="tournament not found")
36	        for rec in records:
37	            row_num = rec["row_num"] + 1  # +1 to count the header row, matching the old wording
38	            err = importer.validate(rec["data"], cfg["cols"], cur)
39	            if err:
40	                errors.append(f"row {row_num}: {err}")
41	                continue
42	            cur.execute("SAVEPOINT row")
43	            try:
44	                cur.execute("SELECT 1 FROM player WHERE usta_number = %s",
45	                            (rec["data"]["usta_number"],))
46	                existed = cur.fetchone() is not None
47	                cfg["merge"](cur, tournament_id, rec["data"])
48	                cur.execute("RELEASE SAVEPOINT row")
49	                if existed:
50	                    updated += 1
51	                else:
52	                    created += 1
53	                upserted += 1
54	            except HTTPException as e:
55	                cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT row")

1	"""Spreadsheet import: stage uploads, then merge into the main tables (audit §3.8).
2	
3	Flow: download a template (CSV/XLSX) -> upload a filled file -> rows land in
4	`import_row` (staged + per-row validated) -> review the summary -> merge the valid
5	rows into the real tables. See app/importer.py for the per-type registry.
6	"""
7	from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, File, HTTPException, Response, UploadFile
8	from psycopg.types.json import Json
9	
10	from .. import importer
11	from ..db import db_dep
12	
13	router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/import", tags=["import"])
14	
15	_XLSX = "application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet"
16	
17	
18	@router.get("/types")
19	def list_types():
20	    return importer.types_meta()
21	
22	
23	@router.get("/template/{import_type}")
24	def download_template(import_type: str, fmt: str = "csv"):
25	    if import_type not in importer.TYPES:
26	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="unknown import type")
27	    if fmt == "xlsx":
28	        body, media, ext = importer.template_xlsx(import_type), _XLSX, "xlsx"
29	    else:
30	        body, media, ext = importer.template_csv(import_type), "text/csv", "csv"
31	    return Response(content=body, media_type=media, headers={
32	        "Content-Disposition": f'attachment; filename="{import_type}-template.{ext}"'})
33	
34	
35	@router.post("/tournaments/{tournament_id}/{import_type}", status_code=201)
36	async def upload(tournament_id: int, import_type: str,
37	                 file: UploadFile = File(...), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
38	    """Parse + validate an uploaded file into a staging batch (no main-table writes)."""
39	    cfg = importer.TYPES.get(import_type)
40	    if cfg is None:
41	        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="unknown import type")
42	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
43	        cur.execute("SELECT id FROM tournament WHERE id = %s", (tournament_id,))
44	        if cur.fetchone() is None:
45	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="tournament not found")
46	        rows = importer.parse_file(file.filename, await file.read(), cfg["cols"])
47	        cur.execute(
48	            "INSERT INTO import_batch (tournament_id, import_type, filename) "
49	            "VALUES (%s,%s,%s) RETURNING id",
50	            (tournament_id, import_type, file.filename),
51	        )
52	        bid = cur.fetchone()["id"]
53	        errors = []
54	        valid = 0
55	        for r in rows:
56	            err = importer.validate(r["data"], cfg["cols"], cur)
57	            if err is None:
58	                valid += 1
59	            else:
60	                errors.append({"row": r["row_num"], "error": err})
61	            cur.execute(
62	                "INSERT INTO import_row (batch_id, row_num, data, valid, error) "
63	                "VALUES (%s,%s,%s,%s,%s)",
64	                (bid, r["row_num"], Json(r["data"]), err is None, err),
65	            )
66	    return {"batch_id": bid, "import_type": import_type, "total": len(rows),
67	            "valid": valid, "invalid": len(rows) - valid, "errors": errors[:50]}
68	
69	
70	@router.get("/batches/{batch_id}")
71	def get_batch(batch_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
72	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
73	        cur.execute("SELECT id, tournament_id, import_type, filename, status, created_at "
74	                    "FROM import_batch WHERE id = %s", (batch_id,))
75	        batch = cur.fetchone()
76	        if batch is None:
77	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="batch not found")
78	        cur.execute("SELECT row_num, data, valid, error, merged FROM import_row "
79	                    "WHERE batch_id = %s ORDER BY row_num", (batch_id,))
80	        batch["rows"] = cur.fetchall()
81	    return batch
82	
83	
84	@router.post("/batches/{batch_id}/merge")
85	def merge_batch(batch_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
86	    """Merge the valid, not-yet-merged rows into the main tables (per-row savepoint)."""
87	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
88	        cur.execute("SELECT tournament_id, import_type, status FROM import_batch WHERE id = %s",
89	                    (batch_id,))
90	        batch = cur.fetchone()
91	        if batch is None:
92	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="batch not found")
93	        merge = importer.TYPES[batch["import_type"]]["merge"]
94	        tid = batch["tournament_id"]
95	        cur.execute("SELECT id, row_num, data FROM import_row "
96	                    "WHERE batch_id = %s AND valid AND NOT merged ORDER BY row_num", (batch_id,))
97	        rows = cur.fetchall()
98	        merged, errors, conflicts = 0, [], []
99	        for r in rows:
100	            cur.execute("SAVEPOINT imp")
101	            try:
102	                note = merge(cur, tid, r["data"])  # conflict note (str) or None
103	                cur.execute("RELEASE SAVEPOINT imp")
104	                # Audit M10: the bookkeeping UPDATE runs outside the merge
105	                # savepoint — wrap it so a constraint hit here doesn't abort
106	                # the whole batch transaction.
107	                cur.execute("SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
108	                try:
109	                    cur.execute("UPDATE import_row SET merged = true, error = %s WHERE id = %s",
110	                                (note, r["id"]))
111	                    cur.execute("RELEASE SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
112	                except Exception:
113	                    cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
114	                merged += 1
115	                if note:
116	                    conflicts.append({"row": r["row_num"], "detail": note})
117	            except HTTPException as e:
118	                # Audit T3: preserve the helpful 400 detail instead of stringifying
119	                # the HTTPException repr (which leaked status codes into the UI).
120	                cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp")
121	                msg = str(e.detail)[:300]
122	                cur.execute("SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
123	                try:
124	                    cur.execute("UPDATE import_row SET error = %s WHERE id = %s", (msg, r["id"]))
125	                    cur.execute("RELEASE SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
126	                except Exception:
127	                    cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
128	                errors.append({"row": r["row_num"], "error": msg})
129	            except Exception as e:  # row-level failure: keep the rest of the batch
130	                cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp")
131	                msg = str(e)[:300]
132	                cur.execute("SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
133	                try:
134	                    cur.execute("UPDATE import_row SET error = %s WHERE id = %s", (msg, r["id"]))
135	                    cur.execute("RELEASE SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
136	                except Exception:
137	                    cur.execute("ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT imp_bk")
138	                errors.append({"row": r["row_num"], "error": msg})
139	        cur.execute("UPDATE import_batch SET status = 'merged' WHERE id = %s", (batch_id,))
140	    return {"merged": merged, "failed": len(errors), "conflicts": conflicts[:50],
141	            "errors": errors[:50]}
142	
143	
144	@router.delete("/batches/{batch_id}", status_code=204)
145	def discard_batch(batch_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
146	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
147	        cur.execute("DELETE FROM import_batch WHERE id = %s", (batch_id,))
148	        if cur.rowcount == 0:
149	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="batch not found")
150	    return Response(status_code=204)

---

**Findings**

The audit has been thorough; remaining items are narrow:

1. **Stale grids after staged-import merge for pairing/doubles/distances.** `_importRefresh()` in `frontend/app.js:1378-1381` reloads only roster/late/withdrawals/sched/divflex/photel. After a successful merge of `pairing_avoidances`, `doubles_requests`, or `distances`, the corresponding grids are not refreshed; the user must navigate away and back. The merge UX otherwise advertises success cleanly, masking the stale view.

2. **No exportCols on pairing/doubles grids.** `pairingGrid` (`app.js:2545`), `doublesReqGrid` (`app.js:2595`), and `doublesPairGrid` (`app.js:2620`) are built via `makeListGrid` without an `exportCols` parameter, so CSV export falls back to `table.download("csv")` — which emits the visible columns (e.g. pairing "Players" formatted string like "Smith, John & Jones, Mary"), not the importer-shaped columns (`usta_1, usta_2, ...`). This breaks the "round-trip via Import page" promise that holds for every other Part-B list.

3. **Pairing/doubles importer: missing-player rows stage as valid, fail at merge.** `importer.validate()` (`importer.py:97-101`) only pre-checks gender for new players when the row carries the canonical column `usta_number`. Pairing rows use `usta_1`…`usta_6`, and doubles `partner_usta` is a bare string, so unknown USTAs slip past staging and only blow up inside `_merge_pairing` / `_merge_doubles` via `upsert_player`'s 400. The TD sees a "0 invalid" staging summary, clicks Merge, and gets per-row errors that were knowable at upload time. Same hole, smaller blast radius, as the gender pre-check that motivated audit T2.

4. **`_merge_distance` silently picks an arbitrary row on duplicate labels.** `importer.py:300-313` looks up an official by `last_name` (and optionally `first_name`) and a site by `name`; when more than one match exists it takes whatever `cur.fetchone()` returns first, with no ORDER BY and no ambiguity error. Two officials with the same last name → distance gets written against one of them, deterministically by storage order but not by intent.

5. **`_merge_distance` advertises `tid` is unused** but the route forces a tournament_id into the URL and the staging row, so a TD must pick an arbitrary tournament before importing a global Setup catalog. Cosmetic / UX, not a bug, but worth a one-line note in the Import page section ("any tournament — distances are global") since the type description in `importer.py:435` already says "(Setup catalog)" but the URL shape contradicts it.

6. **`test_import_merge_per_type_smoke` (`test_smoke.py:413-444`) doesn't cover the conflict-note path.** Each row is unique-and-new, so it never re-imports the same row to verify `_exists` returns a `conflict` note. The distance test does cover the overwrite path; the per-type smoke does not. Names imply it tests the merge contract; in practice it only tests the happy path. Not a bug, but the "catches drift" comment over-promises.

7. **`_merge_distance` `source` whitelist silently downgrades.** `_merge_distance` (`importer.py:329-331`) accepts `manual` or `geocoded` and otherwise rewrites to `manual` with no warning. A row with `source=imported` quietly becomes `manual` and the TD has no way to know. Minor: a conflict-note return on coercion would match the pattern used elsewhere.

Nothing critical. Items 1–3 are real UX regressions stemming from the import/export consolidation; the rest are edge cases.
